source.description: Built-in image generation created a six-panel pencil process contact sheet for the picnic-basket lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet was reviewed before cropping; the final panel is used as the finished asset.

process_strategy: The frames build one consistent basket from a rounded box, then add the handle, cloth, apple, weave bands, and finishing graphite before the final panel.

final_elements.name: rounded basket body
final_elements.description: A squat rounded basket body gives the drawing its main container shape.

final_elements.name: arched handle
final_elements.description: A broad arched handle rises from both sides of the basket.

final_elements.name: folded picnic cloth
final_elements.description: A soft cloth drapes over the basket rim with visible folds.

final_elements.name: small apple
final_elements.description: A single apple peeks from one side above the basket rim.

final_elements.name: woven basket bands
final_elements.description: Horizontal and vertical weave marks wrap across the basket body.

final_elements.name: soft ground shadow
final_elements.description: A pale shadow under the basket anchors it lightly to the page.

frames.visible_job: Place the rounded box shape for the basket body and a light rim guide.
Add the arched handle from one side of the basket to the other.
Draw the folded picnic cloth sitting over the basket rim.
Place the small apple shape and refine the basket rim around it.
Add the woven basket bands and a soft shadow under the basket.

final_step.visible_job: Darken keeper lines, add restrained graphite shading, and clarify the already-drawn weave, cloth folds, apple, and handle.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
